title: acaena × subglabra
intro: Acaena × subglabra, also known as New Zealand caterpillar weed, is a hybrid plant species in the buttercup family, characterized by its spreading habit and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Acaena × subglabra include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for skin conditions and minor injuries. Acaena × subglabra can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
